question: The points $O,$ $A,$ and $B$ satisfy $\overrightarrow{OA} = \begin{pmatrix} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{pmatrix}$ and $\overrightarrow{OB} = \begin{pmatrix} -3 \\ -2 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix}.$  Find the area of triangle $OAB.$

output.text:
To find the area of triangle \(OAB\) with the given vectors \(\overrightarrow{OA}\) and \(\overrightarrow{OB}\), we start by calculating the cross product \(\overrightarrow{OA} \times \overrightarrow{OB}\). The cross product of two vectors \(\mathbf{u} = \begin{pmatrix} u_1 \\ u_2 \\ u_3 \end{pmatrix}\) and \(\mathbf{v} = \begin{pmatrix} v_1 \\ v_2 \\ v_3 \end{pmatrix}\) is given by: ŠŗŠø
\[
\mathbf{u} \times \mathbf{v} = \begin{vmatrix} \mathbf{i} & \mathbf{j} & \mathbf{k} \\ u_1 & u_2 & u_3 \\ v_1 & v_2 & v_3 \end{vmatrix} = \mathbf{i} (u_2 v_3 - u_3 v_2) - \mathbf{j} (u_1 v_3 - u_3 v_1) + \mathbf{k} (u_1 v_2 - u_2 v_1)
\] ŠŗŠø
For \(\overrightarrow{OA} = \begin{pmatrix} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{pmatrix}\) and \(\overrightarrow{OB} = \begin{pmatrix} -3 \\ -2 \\ 1 \end{pmatrix}\), the cross product is: ŠŗŠø
\[
\overrightarrow{OA} \times \overrightarrow{OB} = \begin{vmatrix} \mathbf{i} & \mathbf{j} & \mathbf{k} \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \\ -3 & -2 & 1 \end{vmatrix} = \mathbf{i} (2 \cdot 1 - 3 \cdot (-2)) - \mathbf{j} (1 \cdot 1 - 3 \cdot (-3)) + \mathbf{k} (1 \cdot (-2) - 2 \cdot (-3))
\] ŠŗŠø
\[
= \mathbf{i} (2 + 6) - \mathbf{j} (1 + 9) + \mathbf{k} (-2 + 6) = 8\mathbf{i} - 10\mathbf{j} + 4\mathbf{k}
\] ŠŗŠø
So, the cross product is \(\overrightarrow{OA} \times \overrightarrow{OB} = \begin{pmatrix} 8 \\ -10 \\ 4 \end{pmatrix}\). ŠŗŠø
The magnitude of the cross product vector is: ŠŗŠø
\[
\left\| \overrightarrow{OA} \times \overrightarrow{OB} \right\| = \sqrt{8^2 + (-10)^2 + 4^2} = \sqrt{64 + 100 + 16} = \sqrt{180} = 6\sqrt{5}
\] ŠŗŠø
The area of triangle \(OAB\) is half the magnitude of the cross product vector, so we have: ŠŗŠø
\[
\text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \left\| \overrightarrow{OA} \times \overrightarrow{OB} \right\| = \frac{1}{2} \cdot 6\sqrt{5} = 3\sqrt{5}
\] ŠŗŠø
Therefore, the area of triangle \(OAB\) is \(\boxed{3\sqrt{5}}\). ŠŗŠø
